title: The Psychology of Impulse Spending
description: Quick reward, brief relief, quiet regret.

clips.narration: Your hand is in the cart before the decision feels real.
You point, hesitate, then reach anyway.
The wallet opens with a flutter of doubt.
A hard day makes small treats feel like medicine.
Stress narrows the future to the next five minutes.
You buy the feeling of relief, not the object.
One tap while bored. One tap while anxious. The pattern repeats.
For a moment it works. Then the bill arrives inside your head.
Back at the desk, the purchase does not fix what hurt.
Impulse spending is often emotion spending. Pause at the shelf. Name the feeling. Decide twice.
